commits.message:
[Refactor][Attention] Remove paged attention (#11899)

### What this PR does / why we need it?

This PR removes the temporary Paged Attention (PA) fallback from the
standard Ascend attention backend and routes decode and full-graph
capture/replay through Fused Infer Attention (FIA) only.

Specifically, it:

- removes `pa_shape_list` from `additional_config`;
- removes PA selection, graph capture/replay parameters, workspace
handling, and the direct PA forward path;
- routes regular decode and Gemma4 large-head decode through FIA;
- removes obsolete PA settings from tests, examples, FAQs, and
translated configuration docs;
- updates unit tests to assert that decode uses FIA.

The independent Ascend 310P attention implementation is intentionally
unchanged because it does not currently have an FIA replacement for its
decode and split-fuse paths.

### Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?

Yes. `pa_shape_list` is removed, and the standard Ascend backend no
longer switches to PA for configured runtime batch sizes. Those decode
workloads now use FIA.

### How was this patch tested?

- `bash format.sh ci`
- `python -m py_compile vllm_ascend/ascend_config.py
vllm_ascend/attention/utils.py vllm_ascend/attention/attention_v1.py
vllm_ascend/worker/model_runner_v1.py
tests/ut/attention/a2/test_attention_v1.py
tests/ut/_310p/attention/test_attention_v1_310.py`
- verified that no active `pa_shape_list`, `using_paged_attention`, PA
graph parameter, or standard-backend direct PA operator references
remain

NPU unit, startup, and inference validation are pending because the
local macOS environment does not provide `torch`, `torch_npu`, or
`vllm`. The PR is opened as draft until hardware validation is added.

- vLLM version: v0.23.0
- vLLM main:
https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/commit/1f486d96a17303ce8db8e02be39545b2be338446

---------

Signed-off-by: maoxx241 <maomaoyu870@gmail.com>

 Enable FlashComm_v1 (`VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_FLASHCOMM1=1`) when using Tensor Parallelism (TP ≥ 2) with high concurrency. It is threshold-protected and will not activate in low-concurrency scenarios where it could degrade performance.
-

-FIA (Flash Attention) is the default attention operator in vLLM-Ascend. In some batch-size settings (particularly medium concurrency), FIA may exhibit suboptimal performance. The PA (Page Attention) operator can be manually enabled via `pa_shape_list` in `--additional-config`. When the runtime batch size matches a value in `pa_shape_list`, the framework switches to PA. This is a temporary tuning knob — future FIA optimizations will make this parameter obsolete.

-            # in inference. This will be removed once npu_fused_infer_attention_score
-            # outperforms _npu_paged_attention on all cases.
             if profile_seq_lens is not None:
                 seq_lens = profile_seq_lens
             else:
-                seq_lens = (
-                    SEQ_LEN_WITH_MAX_PA_WORKSPACE
-                    if is_graph_capturing and using_paged_attention(num_tokens, self.vllm_config)
-                    else max_query_len
-                )  # type: ignore[assignment]
+                seq_lens = max_query_len
 
             self.optimistic_seq_lens_cpu[:num_reqs] = seq_lens
             self.optimistic_seq_lens_cpu[num_reqs:].fill_(0)

commits.message:
[CI]main2main 0710 (#11709)

### What this PR does / why we need it?

#### Upgrade baseline

- Update the verified vLLM main anchor from
[`1f486d96a17303ce8db8e02be39545b2be338446`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/commit/1f486d96a17303ce8db8e02be39545b2be338446)
to
[`e5588e49bc2642670116664a7fc4096e27adb179`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/commit/e5588e49bc2642670116664a7fc4096e27adb179).
The full upstream range is available in this
[comparison](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/compare/1f486d96a17303ce8db8e02be39545b2be338446...e5588e49bc2642670116664a7fc4096e27adb179).
- Preserve the vLLM `0.23.0` compatibility lane while adapting the main
lane to the new upstream contracts. Version gates are used only where
the two lanes differ in function signatures, fields, or module
ownership.
- This PR contains direct adaptations for the new anchor window, fixes
for compatibility debt inherited from older anchors, and a required
HunyuanVL forward backport of upstream [vLLM
#47867](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47867). vLLM `#47872`
is the native-processor migration within the anchored range; `#47867` is
the later dependency and registry cleanup. Because `#47867` was merged
after the target anchor, it is not part of the anchored upstream range
and is identified explicitly as a forward backport below.
- The changes are organized below in the same order as the 20 changed
files in this PR. Each item identifies the upstream change, the
downstream adaptation, and why the adaptation is required. Test-only and
formatting-only changes without a one-to-one upstream PR are called out
explicitly.

#### Changes by file

##### 1.
[`.github/vllm-main-verified.commit`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-e05d8ad974ac045f723e44a8f63948981edf3d4bb6e73ce30d7fa43777113c64)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Update the verified main2main anchor | This does not map to a single
upstream PR; it represents the
[`1f486d96...e5588e49`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/compare/1f486d96a17303ce8db8e02be39545b2be338446...e5588e49bc2642670116664a7fc4096e27adb179)
upgrade window. | [Set the anchor to
`e5588e49`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-e05d8ad974ac045f723e44a8f63948981edf3d4bb6e73ce30d7fa43777113c64R1).
| This file is the source of truth used by the main2main workflow to
select the vLLM main revision. |

##### 2.
[`pyproject.toml`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-50c86b7ed8ac2cf95bd48334961bf0530cdc77b5a56f852c5c61b89d735fd711)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Upgrade the build dependency from Transformers `5.5.4` to `5.13.0` |
[vLLM #47867 moves the test dependency to the 5.13
release](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47867/files#diff-fd6b5ddca74f8272b81cfeb1d9ca3cce091e1984c96e3f8f8ed19c840031805fR43);
the functional requirement comes from [vLLM #47872 switching to the
native
`HunYuanVLProcessor`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47872/files#diff-dd96b220187e9eeecc5dc157ec6e67e66e83e1572b9048e99b1522144e3993f5R34).
| [Pin the build dependency to
`transformers==5.13.0`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-50c86b7ed8ac2cf95bd48334961bf0530cdc77b5a56f852c5c61b89d735fd711R20).
| The native HunyuanVL processor API must be available at build time.
`#47867` is a required forward backport beyond the target anchor. |

##### 3.
[`requirements.txt`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-4d7c51b1efe9043e44439a949dfd92e5827321b34082903477fd04876edb7552)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Align the runtime dependency with Transformers `5.13.0` | [The vLLM
#47867 5.13 release
bump](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47867/files#diff-fd6b5ddca74f8272b81cfeb1d9ca3cce091e1984c96e3f8f8ed19c840031805fR43)
and [the vLLM #47872 native processor
migration](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47872/files#diff-dd96b220187e9eeecc5dc157ec6e67e66e83e1572b9048e99b1522144e3993f5R576).
| [Pin the runtime dependency to
`transformers==5.13.0`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-4d7c51b1efe9043e44439a949dfd92e5827321b34082903477fd04876edb7552R38).
| This prevents the build and runtime environments from using different
processor protocols. |

##### 4.
[`tests/e2e/pull_request/one_card/spec_decode/test_extract_hidden_states.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-7fc4e7cfbc83f08a01fdbd53a6bd363ee196437ee557155d8b512ca0fea0c0d6)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Use the connector helper in both version lanes | There is no
one-to-one upstream PR for this test-hardening change. It adopts the
lock-aware [`load_hidden_states()` / `cleanup_hidden_states()`
contract](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/e5588e49bc2642670116664a7fc4096e27adb179/vllm/distributed/kv_transfer/kv_connector/v1/example_hidden_states_connector.py#L45-L76)
already present at the target anchor. | [Use the connector import in
both
lanes](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-7fc4e7cfbc83f08a01fdbd53a6bd363ee196437ee557155d8b512ca0fea0c0d6R37),
[load through the lock-aware
helper](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-7fc4e7cfbc83f08a01fdbd53a6bd363ee196437ee557155d8b512ca0fea0c0d6R127),
and [clean up in a `finally`
block](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-7fc4e7cfbc83f08a01fdbd53a6bd363ee196437ee557155d8b512ca0fea0c0d6R138).
| The v0.23 connector may still be writing the file when the test reads
it directly. Using the connector helper avoids that reader/writer race,
and cleanup in `finally` removes both the data and lock files even when
an assertion fails. |

##### 5.
[`tests/ut/patch/platform/test_patch_deepseek_v4_tool_call_parser.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-7fbf0b3acfff259841b0026a478718e39edd581c0b0a6083c575becd9b01db73)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Restrict the legacy parser-patch tests to v0.23 | [vLLM #45877 removes
the legacy
`DeepSeekV4ToolParser`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/45877/files#diff-88c48d6287edf19515caf80f1506eb84b87aafd41764b6b5d7e0a7c21e172188L7).
| [Skip the module on main and add a mypy ignore to the legacy
import](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-7fbf0b3acfff259841b0026a478718e39edd581c0b0a6083c575becd9b01db73R6).
| Main now uses the unified parser engine, so test collection must not
import a class that no longer exists. |

##### 6.
[`tests/ut/patch/test_hunyuan_vl_processor_compat.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-78358b6d0594477ea6af5e2eab1bd5cd6143d813e7c6fb1f66328e2b9d8c109b)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Add 11 focused tests for the HunyuanVL compatibility layer | [The vLLM
#47872 native processor
migration](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47872) and [the
vLLM #47867 removal of stale processor
registrations](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47867/files#diff-0b207f2e46fa1472fab13098a7dac4d679110722291d36bbefa2a6c9b9e8d7f1L24).
| [Cover temporary aliases and error recovery, installation order, token
schema validation, the native call protocol, fail-closed registry
cleanup, and prompt-update
ownership](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-78358b6d0594477ea6af5e2eab1bd5cd6143d813e7c6fb1f66328e2b9d8c109bR10).
| These tests verify that the compatibility layer has clear version
boundaries, does not leak changes into `sys.modules` or the registry,
and does not silently accept incorrect vocabulary IDs or unknown future
registry replacements. |

##### 7.
[`vllm_ascend/__init__.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-0da2dd8fe39fe53e06f6be0fdb3abf0b19c6bc13d67e1d6e7b3f63267058d84b)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Install the HunyuanVL compatibility layer before importing the model

directly](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47872/files#diff-dd96b220187e9eeecc5dc157ec6e67e66e83e1572b9048e99b1522144e3993f5R34),
and [vLLM #47867 removes the stale registry
entries](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47867/files#diff-0b207f2e46fa1472fab13098a7dac4d679110722291d36bbefa2a6c9b9e8d7f1L61).
| [Call `install_hunyuan_vl_processor_compat()` before importing models
in
`register_model()`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-0da2dd8fe39fe53e06f6be0fdb3abf0b19c6bc13d67e1d6e7b3f63267058d84bR73).
| The legacy import path and registry must be repaired before model
modules are imported, otherwise model registration can fail at import
time. |

##### 8.
[`vllm_ascend/core/single_type_kv_cache_manager.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-faafed87c05636c8b8d1a1296508d2fcd00b006877fc8290bc62e280d68895)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |

changes the manager factory to

| [Accept both the new and legacy budget
arguments](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-faafed87c05636c8b8d1a1296508d2fcd00b006877fc8290bc62e280d68895R247),
then [call `max_admission_blocks_per_request` with the correct
version-specific
keyword](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-faafed87c05636c8b8d1a1296508d2fcd00b006877fc8290bc62e280d68895R291).
| Ascend overrides the upstream factory. Without this adaptation, main
can fail with `TypeError`; using the old per-batch budget on main can
also understate the per-request KV-cache admission bound when batches
overlap under asynchronous scheduling. |

##### 9.
[`vllm_ascend/distributed/kv_transfer/kv_pool/cpu_offload/cpu_kv_cache_manager.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-b16534c3906cacd79f5d4310347e85210182a2002cc66a4e71c14d468a1a0de3)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |

| [Pass `max_num_batched_tokens` on v0.23 and `max_in_flight_tokens` on
main](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-b16534c3906cacd79f5d4310347e85210182a2002cc66a4e71c14d468a1a0de3R79).
| CPU offload must use the same admission semantics as the primary KV
path. |

##### 10.
[`vllm_ascend/patch/hunyuan_vl_processor_compat.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1ebac8fc9d624d6deaea3c0ee308a753930dae2ee09afb32439979e6202ba7f8)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Restore the named-token schema for the legacy HunyuanOCR tokenizer |
[vLLM #47872 switches to the native
processor](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47872/files#diff-dd96b220187e9eeecc5dc157ec6e67e66e83e1572b9048e99b1522144e3993f5R34).
| [Register the image-start, image-end, image, and pad tokens before
native processor initialization and strictly validate their fixed
IDs](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1ebac8fc9d624d6deaea3c0ee308a753930dae2ee09afb32439979e6202ba7f8R37).
| The native API expects named special tokens, while the legacy
HunyuanOCR tokenizer only guarantees the existing vocabulary. The
compatibility layer must bridge the schema without accepting incorrect

| Provide the legacy module paths temporarily while importing v0.23 |
[vLLM #47872 removes the in-tree HunyuanVL processor
modules](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47872/files#diff-c7ad0c42e4131fe0c7403be31dde4f9570c60d6edc245efcd651321d93c1e43bL1).
| [Inject aliases for the native processors only while importing the
v0.23 `hunyuan_vision` module, then restore the module table and
parent-module attributes in
`finally`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1ebac8fc9d624d6deaea3c0ee308a753930dae2ee09afb32439979e6202ba7f8R84).
| The v0.23 model still imports the removed legacy paths. Temporary
aliases solve the import without permanently polluting the global module
table. |
| Remove stale lazy-registry entries | [vLLM #47867 removes the legacy
exports](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47867/files#diff-0b207f2e46fa1472fab13098a7dac4d679110722291d36bbefa2a6c9b9e8d7f1L24)
and [the stale `_CLASS_TO_MODULE`
entries](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47867/files#diff-0b207f2e46fa1472fab13098a7dac4d679110722291d36bbefa2a6c9b9e8d7f1L61).
| [Delete only entries that still point exactly to the legacy modules
and fail closed on unknown
replacements](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1ebac8fc9d624d6deaea3c0ee308a753930dae2ee09afb32439979e6202ba7f8R138).
| The target anchor does not yet include `#47867`, but the dependency is
already upgraded to 5.13. The registry cleanup must therefore be
forward-backported without overwriting a future upstream registration. |
| Align the native processor loader | [vLLM #47872 replaces `use_fast`
with
`backend="pil"`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47872/files#diff-dd96b220187e9eeecc5dc157ec6e67e66e83e1572b9048e99b1522144e3993f5R576).
| [Drop `use_fast`, default to the PIL backend, and instantiate the
compatible native
processor](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1ebac8fc9d624d6deaea3c0ee308a753930dae2ee09afb32439979e6202ba7f8R163).
| Transformers 5.13 uses a different processor-backend protocol. |
| Backport the 5.13 processor call protocol to v0.23 | [vLLM #47872
introduces the new `_call_hf_processor`
protocol](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47872/files#diff-dd96b220187e9eeecc5dc157ec6e67e66e83e1572b9048e99b1522144e3993f5R729).
| [Resolve the processor, wrap image tokens only when they are not
already wrapped, merge the arguments, and call the processor
context](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1ebac8fc9d624d6deaea3c0ee308a753930dae2ee09afb32439979e6202ba7f8R174).
| The v0.23 model class remains in place but must follow the 5.13
processor contract without double-wrapping cached prompts. |
| Keep the installation flow scoped by version | This closes the
combined contracts introduced by `#47872` and `#47867`. | [On v0.23,
install the aliases, registry cleanup, loader, and call-protocol
backport; on main, patch the loader only when stale registry entries are
present](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1ebac8fc9d624d6deaea3c0ee308a753930dae2ee09afb32439979e6202ba7f8R199).
| This preserves the native prompt-update ownership of each lane and
avoids over-patching. |

##### 11.
[`vllm_ascend/patch/platform/__init__.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-929f6388f6c5c50ec4618e78449a657028c871fad35a110b9f32608705102414)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Move registration of the legacy DeepSeekV4 parser patch into the v0.23
gate | [vLLM #45877 removes the legacy
`DeepSeekV4ToolParser`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/45877/files#diff-88c48d6287edf19515caf80f1506eb84b87aafd41764b6b5d7e0a7c21e172188L7).
| [Import the legacy patch only on
v0.23](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-929f6388f6c5c50ec4618e78449a657028c871fad35a110b9f32608705102414R36).
| Main uses the upstream engine parser and must not import a removed
class during initialization. |

##### 12.
[`vllm_ascend/patch/platform/patch_deepseek_v4_tool_call_parser.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-941928c3492306ab0b66ce545e5fccc017acc5c64f02f83a590a9c128b1c8446)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Add a version guard inside the patch module itself | [vLLM #45877
removes the legacy
parser](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/45877/files#diff-88c48d6287edf19515caf80f1506eb84b87aafd41764b6b5d7e0a7c21e172188L7).
| [Raise a clear error if imported outside v0.23 and add a mypy ignore
to the legacy
import](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-941928c3492306ab0b66ce545e5fccc017acc5c64f02f83a590a9c128b1c8446R38).
| This enforces the compatibility boundary at both the registration
layer and the module layer, preventing collection and import failures on
main. |

##### 13.
[`vllm_ascend/patch/platform/patch_kv_cache_coordinator.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1755e8b1b338d10faabf51147a2c693d4398fa66750a594a16ff103a820c9d97)

| Change | Upstream change | Downstream adaptation | Why it is needed |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Normalize the legacy and new KV token budgets | [vLLM #47728 changes
the coordinator budget to
`max_in_flight_tokens`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47728/files#diff-43875c71daa893ef7567e21633d9988c2baf95bef61e3a334a6d584d6444d725R70).
| [Normalize `max_num_batched_tokens` on v0.23 and
`max_in_flight_tokens` on main through
`_select_kv_token_budget`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1755e8b1b338d10faabf51147a2c693d4398fa66750a594a16ff103a820c9d97R34),
then [store the normalized budget in the
coordinator](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1755e8b1b338d10faabf51147a2c693d4398fa66750a594a16ff103a820c9d97R91).
| Under asynchronous scheduling, multiple batches may overlap. Main must
use the in-flight budget so the per-request KV-cache admission bound
accounts for the worst-case overlapping batches. |
| Propagate the KV-zeroing requirement to per-spec managers | [vLLM
#44490 records blocks for zeroing only when zeroing is
required](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/44490/files#diff-43875c71daa893ef7567e21633d9988c2baf95bef61e3a334a6d584d6444d725R118).
| [Propagate `needs_kv_cache_zeroing` only on
main](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1755e8b1b338d10faabf51147a2c693d4398fa66750a594a16ff103a820c9d97R134).
| The Ascend override hides the upstream propagation point. Without it,
block IDs can accumulate when no consumer needs them, while v0.23 cannot
accept the new argument. |
| Adapt every coordinator-construction branch | [The new coordinator
factory signature in vLLM
#47728](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47728/files#diff-43875c71daa893ef7567e21633d9988c2baf95bef61e3a334a6d584d6444d725R70).
| [Accept both entry-point
signatures](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1755e8b1b338d10faabf51147a2c693d4398fa66750a594a16ff103a820c9d97R471),
[select the version-specific keyword for the original single-group
branch](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-1755e8b1b338d10faabf51147a2c693d4398fa66750a594a16ff103a820c9d97R513),
and pass the normalized budget through the DeepSeekV4 and custom hybrid
branches. | Ascend overrides the complete coordinator construction
chain. Missing any branch can cause `TypeError` or underestimate the
asynchronous in-flight KV budget. |

##### 14.
[`vllm_ascend/worker/block_table.py`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/pull/11709/files#diff-169b5fefc2e637b704ed89e92b392e7a18478bfb9b8ddd1134faeeaa400f3de7)

#### Compatibility and review notes

- Version gates in this PR are limited to real contract differences
between main and v0.23.0; no unrelated cleanup is included.
- The prefix-cache event behavior from [vLLM
#45261](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/45261) remains fully
owned by the upstream manager, so static review confirmed that no
downstream change is required.
- The retrospective also identified four static candidates that still
require support-scope confirmation or focused testing: multi-buffer
concurrency in `KVBlockZeroer`
([#48085](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/48085)), MRV2
uniform-decode ordering
([#47381](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/47381)), Tensor
causality ([#48135](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/48135)),
and `mm_req_doc_ranges` propagation
([#46942](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/46942)). These are
not reproduced or fixed issues in this PR, and green CI alone does not
prove that the paths are unreachable.
- The links above are based on PR head
[`8092d3f66599ce07cd0aca2bcc99d14b8a9192f8`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/commit/8092d3f66599ce07cd0aca2bcc99d14b8a9192f8).
If more commits are pushed, the head reference and any shifted PR Files
line anchors should be refreshed.

### Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?

Yes. This is a compatibility and dependency update, not a new
Ascend-specific public API.

- The main lane advances to vLLM `e5588e49` and adopts its
version-specific contracts for per-request KV-cache admission, deferred
asynchronous-output Events, `InputBatch`/MRV2, and DFlash, together with
the narrowly scoped HunyuanVL dependency and registry backport described
above.
- The build and runtime dependency on Transformers is upgraded to
`5.13.0`.
- The vLLM `0.23.0` compatibility lane remains supported through
narrowly scoped version gates. DFlash is rejected explicitly on v0.23.0
with `NotImplementedError` instead of failing during import or silently
falling back.

### How was this patch tested?

- [E2E workflow run
29188017361](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/actions/runs/29188017361)
completed successfully on PR head
[`8092d3f66599ce07cd0aca2bcc99d14b8a9192f8`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-ascend/commit/8092d3f66599ce07cd0aca2bcc99d14b8a9192f8).
- `lint-and-select-tests` passed, including pre-commit, mypy,
coverage-configuration validation, and selected-test discovery.
- All 14 selected-test matrix jobs for vLLM `0.23.0` passed.
- All 14 selected-test matrix jobs for the vLLM main anchor `e5588e49`
passed.
- The matrix covered CPU, single-card A2, 2-card and 4-card A3, and
1-card and 4-card 310P runners.
- The workflow contained 30 jobs: 29 succeeded, and the only skipped job
was `upload-coverage-to-obs`. This PR therefore does not claim a
coverage result.

- vLLM version: v0.23.0
- vLLM main:
https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/commit/1f486d96a17303ce8db8e02be39545b2be338446

+# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
+
+import sys
+from types import ModuleType, SimpleNamespace
+from typing import Any
+
+import pytest
+
+import vllm_ascend.patch.hunyuan_vl_processor_compat as compat
+
+
+def test_v023_imports_native_processors_without_persistent_aliases(monkeypatch):
+    import transformers.models.hunyuan_vl.image_processing_hunyuan_vl as native_image
+    import vllm.transformers_utils.processors as vllm_processors
+
+    class FakeProcessor:
+        pass
+
+    class FakeImageProcessor:
+        pass
+
+    def fake_smart_resize(*_args: Any, **_kwargs: Any) -> tuple[int, int]:
+        return (1, 1)
+
+    processor_module_name = compat._STALE_PROCESSOR_MODULES["HunYuanVLProcessor"]
+    image_module_name = compat._STALE_PROCESSOR_MODULES["HunYuanVLImageProcessor"]
+    previous_modules = {
+        processor_module_name: sys.modules.get(processor_module_name),
+        image_module_name: sys.modules.get(image_module_name),
+    }
+    previous_attributes = {
+        "hunyuan_vl": vars(vllm_processors).get("hunyuan_vl"),
+        "hunyuan_vl_image": vars(vllm_processors).get("hunyuan_vl_image"),
+    }
+    hunyuan_vision = ModuleType("vllm.model_executor.models.hunyuan_vision")
+
+    def import_hunyuan_vision(name: str) -> ModuleType:
+        assert name == hunyuan_vision.__name__
+        assert sys.modules[processor_module_name].HunYuanVLProcessor is FakeProcessor
+        assert sys.modules[image_module_name].HunYuanVLImageProcessor is FakeImageProcessor
+        assert sys.modules[image_module_name].smart_resize is fake_smart_resize
+        vars(vllm_processors)["hunyuan_vl"] = sys.modules[processor_module_name]
+        vars(vllm_processors)["hunyuan_vl_image"] = sys.modules[image_module_name]
+        return hunyuan_vision
+
+    monkeypatch.setattr(compat, "HunYuanVLProcessor", FakeProcessor)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(native_image, "HunYuanVLImageProcessor", FakeImageProcessor)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(native_image, "smart_resize", fake_smart_resize)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(compat.importlib, "import_module", import_hunyuan_vision)
+
+    assert compat._import_v023_hunyuan_vision() is hunyuan_vision
+
+    for module_name, previous_module in previous_modules.items():
+        assert sys.modules.get(module_name) is previous_module
+    for attribute_name, previous_attribute in previous_attributes.items():
+        assert vars(vllm_processors).get(attribute_name) is previous_attribute
+
+
+def test_v023_restores_aliases_after_import_error(monkeypatch):
+    import transformers.models.hunyuan_vl.image_processing_hunyuan_vl as native_image
+
+    class FakeProcessor:
+        pass
+
+    class FakeImageProcessor:
+        pass
+
+    processor_module_name = compat._STALE_PROCESSOR_MODULES["HunYuanVLProcessor"]
+    image_module_name = compat._STALE_PROCESSOR_MODULES["HunYuanVLImageProcessor"]
+    previous_modules = {
+        processor_module_name: sys.modules.get(processor_module_name),
+        image_module_name: sys.modules.get(image_module_name),
+    }
+
+    monkeypatch.setattr(compat, "HunYuanVLProcessor", FakeProcessor)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(native_image, "HunYuanVLImageProcessor", FakeImageProcessor)
+
+    def fail_import(_name: str) -> ModuleType:
+        raise ImportError("expected test failure")
+
+    monkeypatch.setattr(compat.importlib, "import_module", fail_import)
+
+    with pytest.raises(ImportError, match="expected test failure"):
+        compat._import_v023_hunyuan_vision()
+
+    for module_name, previous_module in previous_modules.items():
+        assert sys.modules.get(module_name) is previous_module
+
+
+def test_installer_runs_v023_backports_in_order(monkeypatch):
+    hunyuan_vision = object()
+    calls: list[Any] = []
+
+    def import_hunyuan_vision() -> object:
+        calls.append("import")
+        return hunyuan_vision
+
+    def clean_registry() -> bool:
+        calls.append("registry")
+        return True
+
+    def patch_processor(module: Any) -> None:
+        calls.append(("processor", module))
+
+    def patch_loader(module: Any) -> None:
+        calls.append(("loader", module))
+
+    monkeypatch.setattr(compat, "vllm_version_is", lambda version: version == "0.23.0")
+    monkeypatch.setattr(
+        compat,
+        "_import_v023_hunyuan_vision",
+        import_hunyuan_vision,
+    )
+    monkeypatch.setattr(
+        compat,
+        "_remove_stale_registry_entries",
+        clean_registry,
+    )
+    monkeypatch.setattr(
+        compat,
+        "_patch_hunyuan_processor_loader",
+        patch_loader,
+    )
+    monkeypatch.setattr(
+        compat,
+        "_patch_v023_processor_methods",
+        patch_processor,
+    )
+    compat.install_hunyuan_vl_processor_compat()
+
+    assert calls == [
+        "import",
+        "registry",
+        ("loader", hunyuan_vision),
+        ("processor", hunyuan_vision),
+    ]
+
+
+def test_installer_cleans_main_registry_before_model_patch(monkeypatch):
+    import vllm.model_executor.models as vllm_models
+
+    def native_get_prompt_updates(*_args: Any, **_kwargs: Any) -> str:
+        return "native"
+
+    class FakeMultiModalProcessor:
+        _get_prompt_updates = native_get_prompt_updates
+
+    hunyuan_vision = SimpleNamespace(
+        HunYuanVLMultiModalProcessor=FakeMultiModalProcessor,
+    )
+    calls: list[Any] = []
+
+    def clean_registry() -> bool:
+        calls.append("registry")
+        return True
+
+    def patch_loader(module: Any) -> None:
+        calls.append(("loader", module))
+
+    monkeypatch.setattr(compat, "vllm_version_is", lambda _version: False)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(
+        compat,
+        "_remove_stale_registry_entries",
+        clean_registry,
+    )
+    monkeypatch.setattr(vllm_models, "hunyuan_vision", hunyuan_vision, raising=False)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(
+        compat,
+        "_patch_hunyuan_processor_loader",
+        patch_loader,
+    )
+    compat.install_hunyuan_vl_processor_compat()
+
+    assert calls == [
+        "registry",
+        ("loader", hunyuan_vision),
+    ]
+    assert FakeMultiModalProcessor._get_prompt_updates is native_get_prompt_updates
+
+
+def test_registers_hunyuan_tokenizer_schema_without_changing_ids():
+    class FakeTokenizer:
+        pad_token = compat._HUNYUAN_VL_SPECIAL_TOKENS["pad_token"]
+        pad_token_id = compat._HUNYUAN_VL_SPECIAL_TOKEN_IDS["pad_token"]
+
+        def __init__(self) -> None:
+            self.registrations: list[dict[str, str]] = []
+
+        def _set_model_specific_special_tokens(self, special_tokens: dict[str, str]) -> None:
+            self.registrations.append(special_tokens)
+            for name, token in special_tokens.items():
+                setattr(self, name, token)
+                setattr(self, f"{name}_id", compat._HUNYUAN_VL_SPECIAL_TOKEN_IDS[name])
+
+    tokenizer = FakeTokenizer()
+

+def test_v023_backports_native_processor_call_protocol(monkeypatch):

+
+    processor_args: list[tuple[Any, dict[str, Any]]] = []
+
+    def get_processor(processor_class: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> object:
+        processor_args.append((processor_class, kwargs))
+        return object()
+
+    processing_info = SimpleNamespace(

+    calls: list[tuple[Any, dict[str, Any], dict[str, Any]]] = []
+    hf_processor = SimpleNamespace(
+        image_token="<image>",

+        image_end_token="<image_end>",

+

+        calls.append((processor, inputs, kwargs))
+        return "processed"
+
+    processor = SimpleNamespace(

+
+    assert (

+
+    wrapped_prompt = "before<image_start><image><image_end>after"
+    assert (

+            {"images": [object()]},
+            {},
+            {},
+        )
+        == "processed"
+    )
+    assert calls[1][1]["text"] == wrapped_prompt
+

+def test_main_removes_only_stale_registry_entries(monkeypatch):

+# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import importlib
+import sys
+from collections.abc import Mapping
+from types import ModuleType
+from typing import Any, cast
+
+from transformers import HunYuanVLProcessor
+
+from vllm_ascend.utils import vllm_version_is
+
+_STALE_PROCESSOR_MODULES = {
+    "HunYuanVLProcessor": "vllm.transformers_utils.processors.hunyuan_vl",
+    "HunYuanVLImageProcessor": "vllm.transformers_utils.processors.hunyuan_vl_image",
+}
+
+_HUNYUAN_VL_EXTRA_SPECIAL_TOKENS = {
+    "image_start_token": "<｜hy_place▁holder▁no▁100｜>",
+    "image_end_token": "<｜hy_place▁holder▁no▁101｜>",
+    "image_token": "<｜hy_place▁holder▁no▁102｜>",
+}
+_HUNYUAN_VL_SPECIAL_TOKENS = {
+    **_HUNYUAN_VL_EXTRA_SPECIAL_TOKENS,
+    "pad_token": "<｜hy_▁pad▁｜>",
+}
+_HUNYUAN_VL_SPECIAL_TOKEN_IDS = {
+    "image_start_token": 120118,
+    "image_end_token": 120119,
+    "image_token": 120120,
+    "pad_token": 120002,
+}
+
+
+def _register_hunyuan_tokenizer_special_tokens(tokenizer: Any) -> None:
+    """Restore the named-token schema required by Transformers 5.13."""
+    missing_tokens = {
+        name: token
+        for name, token in _HUNYUAN_VL_EXTRA_SPECIAL_TOKENS.items()
+        if tokenizer is not None and getattr(tokenizer, name, None) is None
+    }
+    if missing_tokens:
+        tokenizer._set_model_specific_special_tokens(special_tokens=missing_tokens)
+
+    actual_tokens = {
+        name: (getattr(tokenizer, name, None), getattr(tokenizer, f"{name}_id", None))
+        for name in _HUNYUAN_VL_SPECIAL_TOKENS
+    }
+    expected_tokens = {
+        name: (token, _HUNYUAN_VL_SPECIAL_TOKEN_IDS[name]) for name, token in _HUNYUAN_VL_SPECIAL_TOKENS.items()
+    }
+    if actual_tokens != expected_tokens:
+        raise ValueError(
+            "HunyuanVL tokenizer special-token schema does not match the model vocabulary: "
+            f"expected {expected_tokens!r}, got {actual_tokens!r}"
+        )
+
+
+class _HunYuanVLProcessorCompat(HunYuanVLProcessor):
+    """Native processor with the legacy HunyuanOCR token schema restored."""
+
+    def __init__(
+        self,
+        image_processor: Any = None,
+        tokenizer: Any = None,
+        chat_template: Any = None,
+        cat_extra_token: bool = True,
+        **kwargs: Any,
+    ) -> None:
+        _register_hunyuan_tokenizer_special_tokens(tokenizer)
+        super().__init__(
+            image_processor=image_processor,
+            tokenizer=tokenizer,
+            chat_template=chat_template,
+            cat_extra_token=cat_extra_token,
+            **kwargs,
+        )
+
+
+def _import_v023_hunyuan_vision() -> Any:
+    """Import v0.23's model with the native processors from vLLM PR #47872."""
+    from transformers.models.hunyuan_vl.image_processing_hunyuan_vl import (
+        HunYuanVLImageProcessor,
+        smart_resize,
+    )
+
+    aliases = {
+        _STALE_PROCESSOR_MODULES["HunYuanVLProcessor"]: {
+            "HunYuanVLProcessor": HunYuanVLProcessor,
+        },
+        _STALE_PROCESSOR_MODULES["HunYuanVLImageProcessor"]: {
+            "HunYuanVLImageProcessor": HunYuanVLImageProcessor,
+            "smart_resize": smart_resize,
+        },
+    }
+    missing = object()
+    previous_modules = {name: sys.modules.get(name, missing) for name in aliases}
+    parent_module = sys.modules.get("vllm.transformers_utils.processors")
+    previous_attributes = {
+        name.rpartition(".")[2]: (
+            vars(parent_module).get(name.rpartition(".")[2], missing) if parent_module is not None else missing
+        )
+        for name in aliases
+    }
+
+    alias_modules = {}
+    for module_name, exports in aliases.items():
+        module = ModuleType(module_name)
+        module.__package__ = module_name.rpartition(".")[0]
+        module.__dict__.update(exports)
+        module.__dict__["__all__"] = list(exports)
+        alias_modules[module_name] = module
+        sys.modules[module_name] = module
+
+    try:
+        return importlib.import_module("vllm.model_executor.models.hunyuan_vision")
+    finally:
+        for module_name, alias_module in alias_modules.items():
+            previous_module = previous_modules[module_name]
+            if previous_module is missing:
+                if sys.modules.get(module_name) is alias_module:
+                    del sys.modules[module_name]
+            else:
+                sys.modules[module_name] = cast(ModuleType, previous_module)
+
+        current_parent_module = sys.modules.get("vllm.transformers_utils.processors")
+        if current_parent_module is not None:
+            for attribute_name, previous_attribute in previous_attributes.items():
+                if previous_attribute is missing:
+                    if vars(current_parent_module).get(attribute_name) in alias_modules.values():
+                        delattr(current_parent_module, attribute_name)
+                else:
+                    setattr(current_parent_module, attribute_name, previous_attribute)
+
+
+def _remove_stale_registry_entries() -> bool:
+    """Backport the lazy-registry cleanup from vLLM PR #47867."""
+    import vllm.transformers_utils.processors as vllm_processors
+
+    class_to_module = vllm_processors._CLASS_TO_MODULE
+    exported_names = vllm_processors.__all__
+    entries_to_remove = []
+    for class_name, stale_module in _STALE_PROCESSOR_MODULES.items():
+        registered_module = class_to_module.get(class_name)
+        if registered_module is None:
+            continue
+        if registered_module != stale_module:
+            raise RuntimeError(f"Unexpected vLLM processor registry entry for {class_name}: {registered_module!r}")
+        if class_name not in exported_names:
+            raise RuntimeError(f"Missing vLLM processor export for {class_name}")
+
+        entries_to_remove.append(class_name)
+
+    for class_name in entries_to_remove:
+        del class_to_module[class_name]
+        exported_names.remove(class_name)
+
+    return bool(entries_to_remove)
+
+
+def _patch_hunyuan_processor_loader(hunyuan_vision: Any) -> None:
+    """Use the native processor with the complete Hunyuan tokenizer schema."""
+
+    def get_hf_processor(self: Any, **kwargs: object) -> Any:
+        kwargs.pop("use_fast", None)
+        kwargs.setdefault("backend", "pil")
+        return self.ctx.get_hf_processor(_HunYuanVLProcessorCompat, **kwargs)
+
+    hunyuan_vision.HunYuanVLProcessingInfo.get_hf_processor = get_hf_processor
+
+
+def _patch_v023_processor_methods(hunyuan_vision: Any) -> None:
+    """Backport the Transformers 5.13 call protocol from vLLM PR #47872."""
+
+    def call_hf_processor(
+        self: Any,
+        prompt: str,
+        mm_data: Mapping[str, object],
+        mm_kwargs: Mapping[str, object],
+        tok_kwargs: Mapping[str, object],
+    ) -> Any:
+        hf_processor = self.info.get_hf_processor(**mm_kwargs)
+        if mm_data.get("images") is not None and prompt:
+            image_token = hf_processor.image_token
+            wrapped_token = f"{hf_processor.image_start_token}{image_token}{hf_processor.image_end_token}"
+            if image_token in prompt and wrapped_token not in prompt:
+                prompt = prompt.replace(image_token, wrapped_token)
+        return self.info.ctx.call_hf_processor(
+            hf_processor,
+            dict(text=prompt, **mm_data),
+            dict(**mm_kwargs, **tok_kwargs),
+        )
+
+    hunyuan_vision.HunYuanVLMultiModalProcessor._call_hf_processor = call_hf_processor
+
+
+def install_hunyuan_vl_processor_compat() -> None:
+    """Align both supported vLLM refs with Transformers 5.13 Hunyuan APIs."""
+    # Keep each target's native, image-token-only prompt replacement. The
+    # cached processor path applies it inside an existing start/image/end
+    # wrapper; using a full-wrapper replacement here would duplicate wrappers.
+    if vllm_version_is("0.23.0"):
+        v023_hunyuan_vision = _import_v023_hunyuan_vision()
+        _remove_stale_registry_entries()
+        _patch_hunyuan_processor_loader(v023_hunyuan_vision)
+        _patch_v023_processor_methods(v023_hunyuan_vision)
+        return
+
+    if not _remove_stale_registry_entries():
+        return
+    from vllm.model_executor.models import hunyuan_vision as main_hunyuan_vision
+
+    _patch_hunyuan_processor_loader(main_hunyuan_vision)

[TEST]Add A3 multi-machine PD separation test case (#11855)

### What this PR does / why we need it?
Add A3 multi-machine PD separation test case

- vLLM version: v0.23.0
- vLLM main:
https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/commit/1f486d96a17303ce8db8e02be39545b2be338446

Signed-off-by: zxinyue <1264904584@qq.com>
Signed-off-by: taozi01319 <68981779+taozi01319@users.noreply.github.com>

+test_name: "DeepSeek_V3.1T_MTP1_128K_1K_PD"
+model: "Eco-Tech/DeepSeek-V3.1-Terminus-w8a8-mtp-QuaRot"
+num_nodes: 4
+npu_per_node: 16
+
+routing:
+  type: "disaggregated_prefill"
+  groups:
+    prefiller: [ 0, 1 ]
+    decoder: [ 2, 3 ]
+
+config:
+  - node_index: 0
+    port_start: 7100
+    dp_rpc_port: 12321
+    dp_size: 2
+    dp_size_local: 2
+    dp_rank_start: 0
+    tp_size: 8
+    dp_address: "${NODE_0_IP}"
+
+  - node_index: 1
+    port_start: 7100
+    dp_rpc_port: 12321
+    dp_size: 2
+    dp_size_local: 2
+    dp_rank_start: 0
+    tp_size: 8
+    dp_address: "${NODE_1_IP}"
+
+  - node_index: 2
+    port_start: 7100
+    dp_rpc_port: 12321
+    dp_size: 32
+    dp_size_local: 16
+    dp_rank_start: 0
+    tp_size: 1
+    dp_address: "${NODE_2_IP}"
+
+  - node_index: 3
+    port_start: 7100
+    dp_rpc_port: 12321
+    dp_size: 32
+    dp_size_local: 16
+    dp_rank_start: 16
+    tp_size: 1
+    dp_address: "${NODE_2_IP}"
+
+env_common: &env_common
+  SERVER_PORT: "${PORT}"
+  ASCEND_RT_VISIBLE_DEVICES: "${VISIBLE_DEVICES}"
+  VLLM_RPC_TIMEOUT: "3600000"
+  VLLM_EXECUTE_MODEL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: "30000"
+  HCCL_EXEC_TIMEOUT: "204"
+  HCCL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT: "400"
+  LD_PRELOAD: "/usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libjemalloc.so.2:$LD_PRELOAD"
+  OMP_PROC_BIND: "false"
+  OMP_NUM_THREADS: "10"
+  PYTORCH_NPU_ALLOC_CONF: "expandable_segments:True"
+  TASK_QUEUE_ENABLE: 1
+  HCCL_OP_EXPANSION_MODE: "AIV"
+  VLLM_USE_V1: "1"
+  ASCEND_BUFFER_POOL: "4:8"
+  LD_LIBRARY_PATH: "/usr/local/Ascend/ascend-toolkit/latest/python/site-packages/mooncake:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"
+
+env_prefill: &env_prefill
+  <<: *env_common
+  HCCL_BUFFSIZE: "256"
+
+env_decode: &env_decode
+  <<: *env_common
+  HCCL_BUFFSIZE: "1200"
+  VLLM_ASCEND_ENABLE_MLAPO: "1"
+
+templates:
+  - node_index: 0
+    envs:
+      <<: *env_prefill
+    server_cmd_template:
+      - --host
+      - "0.0.0.0"
+      - --port
+      - $SERVER_PORT
+      - --data-parallel-size
+      - ${DP_SIZE}
+      - --data-parallel-rank
+      - ${DP_RANK}
+      - --data-parallel-address
+      - ${DP_ADDRESS}
+      - --data-parallel-rpc-port
+      - ${DP_RPC_PORT}
+      - --tensor-parallel-size
+      - ${TP_SIZE}
+      - --enable-expert-parallel
+      - --seed
+      - "1024"
+      - --max-model-len
+      - "135000"
+      - --max-num-batched-tokens
+      - "1024"
+      - --max-num-seqs
+      - "1"
+      - --enforce-eager
+      - --trust-remote-code
+      - --gpu-memory-utilization
+      - "0.93"
+      - --quantization
+      - "ascend"
+      - --no-enable-prefix-caching
+      - --speculative-config
+      - '{"method": "deepseek_mtp", "num_speculative_tokens": 1}'
+      - --additional-config
+      - '{"recompute_scheduler_enable":false,"enable_shared_expert_dp": true}'
+      - --kv-transfer-config
+      - '{"kv_connector": "MooncakeConnectorV1", "kv_role": "kv_producer", "kv_port": "30000", "engine_id": "0", "kv_connector_extra_config": {"prefill": {"dp_size": 2, "tp_size": 8}, "decode": {"dp_size": 32, "tp_size": 1}}}'
+
+  - node_index: 1
+    envs:
+      <<: *env_prefill
+    server_cmd_template:
+      - --host
+      - "0.0.0.0"
+      - --port
+      - $SERVER_PORT
+      - --data-parallel-size
+      - ${DP_SIZE}
+      - --data-parallel-rank
+      - ${DP_RANK}
+      - --data-parallel-address
+      - ${DP_ADDRESS}
+      - --data-parallel-rpc-port
+      - ${DP_RPC_PORT}
+      - --tensor-parallel-size
+      - ${TP_SIZE}
+      - --enable-expert-parallel
+      - --seed
+      - "1024"
+      - --max-model-len
+      - "135000"
+      - --max-num-batched-tokens
+      - "1024"
+      - --max-num-seqs
+      - "1"
+      - --enforce-eager
+      - --trust-remote-code
+      - --gpu-memory-utilization
+      - "0.93"
+      - --quantization
+      - "ascend"
+      - --no-enable-prefix-caching
+      - --speculative-config
+      - '{"method": "deepseek_mtp", "num_speculative_tokens": 1}'
+      - --additional-config
+      - '{"recompute_scheduler_enable":false,"enable_shared_expert_dp": true}'
+      - --kv-transfer-config
+      - '{"kv_connector": "MooncakeConnectorV1", "kv_role": "kv_producer", "kv_port": "30100", "engine_id": "1", "kv_connector_extra_config": {"prefill": {"dp_size": 2, "tp_size": 8}, "decode": {"dp_size": 32, "tp_size": 1}}}'
+
+  - node_index: 2
+    envs:
+      <<: *env_decode
+    server_cmd_template:
+      - --host
+      - "0.0.0.0"
+      - --port
+      - $SERVER_PORT
